subtitle: The Card That Hasn't Been Drawn Yet
format_note: STUB: this card has no painted art in this build. Hooks file reserves the unlock keys and cross-references so the network around it stays consistent; full content arrives when the art does.

ciphers.text: the Wheel has not turned yet — chapter X exists as a position the lattice expects but the deck has not filled
ciphers.encoding_hint: X sits between IX Hermit (chosen solitude) and XI Justice (one of the THREE REFUSED). Tarot tradition: fate, the turn, the cycle that does not consult. Modern Mythology has not painted the turning yet — but every chapter from VIII onward has acted as if the wheel were already in motion. The placeholder card is the deck admitting that motion without yet rendering it.

easter_eggs: the Wheel is the only chapter in vol5 not yet painted. Every other major card has art; X stays open. The user has not generated it. The deck is conscious of the gap.

X sits between two cards about stillness — Hermit's chosen, Justice's refused. The Wheel is the cycle between two refusals to participate; that the Wheel itself is absent in this build is the deck's truest reading of fate.

X is also one of the four 'card without a face' positions in the deck: VI (Lovers, face-down), X (Wheel, not painted), XI (Justice, refused), XX (Judgement, refused). Four refusals to render — close to a quartile of the Majors.

cross_references.between_chosen_and_refused: hermit.json (IX, chosen solitude) and justice.json (XI, refused reading)
cross_references.uncast_kin: lovers.json::lov_uncast_kin (VI / XI / XX all refused or unrendered)
